# WazzapAgents

> WhatsApp AI agent system ‚Äî Baileys v7 gateway + LLM bridge with automated conversation, moderation, and interactive features.

## What this is

WazzapAgents connects WhatsApp accounts to LLM services (OpenAI-compatible APIs) via a two-process architecture: a Node.js gateway handles the WhatsApp socket (Baileys v7) and a Python bridge handles LLM routing, response generation, and moderation decisions. Communication between the two processes runs over WebSocket: the Node gateway is the WS **server** (binds `WS_BIND_HOST:WS_LISTEN_PORT`, default `127.0.0.1:3000`) and each Python `WaSocket` is a **client** that dials it at `NODE_URL` (default `ws://localhost:3000`), announcing its tenant `folder_path`. The gateway supports multiple WhatsApp accounts (tenants), one per `folder_path`, each fully isolated.

## Stack

- **Node.js 18+** (ESM) ‚Äî WhatsApp gateway via Baileys v7; WebSocket **server**
- **Python 3.10+** ‚Äî LLM bridge with LangChain / ChatOpenAI; WaSocket **client(s)**
- **SQLite** (5 per-tenant DBs) ‚Äî settings.db, stats.db, moderation.db, subagent.db, stickers.db (under `<folder_path>/db`)
- **WebSocket** ‚Äî Node ‚Üî Python JSON protocol (Python clients dial `NODE_URL`)

## Architecture

```
WhatsApp ‚Üê‚Üí Node.js Gateway (Baileys v7, WS SERVER on WS_LISTEN_PORT)
                  ‚Üï  WebSocket  (Python clients dial NODE_URL)
            Python Bridge (LLM1 router ‚Üí LLM2 responder, WaSocket client)
```

- **Node Gateway** (`src/`): WS server, WhatsApp socket lifecycle, inbound message normalization, outbound send/react/delete/kick, slash commands, interactive messages
- **Python Bridge** (`python/bridge/`): WaSocket client that dials `NODE_URL`; message debounce/batching, LLM1 decision routing, LLM2 response generation, tool call extraction, action dispatch back to Node
- **LLM1**: Cheap, fast model decides whether to respond (skip/express-only/respond)
- **LLM2**: Full model generates replies and invokes tools (reply, delete, mute, kick)
- **Protocol**: After the `hello`/`hello_ack` handshake, actions flow Python‚ÜíNode (`send_message`, `react_message`, `delete_message`, `kick_member`, etc.) and events/control events/acks flow Node‚ÜíPython (`incoming_message`, `whatsapp_status`, `action_ack`, etc.); every Node‚ÜíPython frame carries `folderPath`

## Docs

- `AGENTS.md` ‚Äî Full developer context for AI coding agents
- `README.md` ‚Äî Setup, deployment, and WebSocket protocol reference
- `docs/llm-architecture/` ‚Äî Detailed architecture docs (00‚Äì05 series)
- `.env.example` ‚Äî All environment variables with descriptions
